channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Cross-Education Effect on Force Production Capacity After Unilateral Resistance Training in Clinical Populations: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis found that the cross-education effect after unilateral resistance training consistently produces force production gains in the contralateral non-trained muscle across various clinical populations, including those with musculoskeletal conditions and hemiparesis after stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Psychometric Properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale Used After Stroke: A Systematic Review Using COSMIN Methodology.
item.description: A systematic review using COSMIN methodology evaluated the psychometric properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale (TIS) in individuals with stroke, providing clarity on its measurement quality and certainty of evidence.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [synopsis].
item.description: This critically appraised paper indicates that mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as, and health status more than, center-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) led to greater muscle strength gains (SMD=0.48, 95% CI 0.32 to 0.65) and pain reduction (SMD=-0.50, 95% CI -0.76 to -0.24) compared to low-load exercise (LLE) alone, with similar effects on muscle strength compared to high-load exercise (HLE) (SMD= -0.05, 95% CI -0.16 to 0.05).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial (N=105) compared agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between physiotherapists and resident physicians against senior physicians for non-urgent musculoskeletal conditions in an emergency department. The study found high agreement, supporting Advanced Physiotherapy Practice (APP) roles.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: Researchers developed a core set of 9 patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy, grouped into three domains: functioning (physical, emotional, social), health status, and symptoms (pain, fatigue, sleep). This set is suitable for daily practice, research, and quality assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access | APTA
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. significantly outpaces access, identifying key challenges in workforce, access, and awareness.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Physiotherapist-led treatment for femoroacetabular impingement syndrome (the PhysioFIRST study): an assessor-blinded, limited disclosure randomised controlled trial
item.description: The PhysioFIRST study, an assessor-blinded RCT, found physiotherapist-led targeted strengthening (STRENGTH) and standardized stretching (STRETCH) similarly improved hip-related quality of life and patient-perceived Global Rating of Change at 6 months for femoroacetabular impingement syndrome.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Comparative effectiveness of physical therapy interventions in adults with rotator cuff tendinopathy: a systematic review and network meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and network meta-analysis compared physical therapy interventions for rotator cuff tendinopathy, aiming to identify the most effective intervention for pain, function, quality of life (QoL), and adverse events (AEs).
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Patterns of Outcome Measure Utilization in Stroke Rehabilitation Lower Extremity Trials Conducted in Low-to-Middle versus High-Income Countries.
item.description: This review (N=1,548 RCTs) found that low-to-middle income countries (LMICs) used more generalized impairment and activities of daily living outcome measures relying on patient observation in stroke rehabilitation lower extremity trials, compared to high-income countries (HICs) that favored direct patient participation measures.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Anti-Compensatory Saccades Changes After Visuo-Vestibular Physical Therapy in People With Acute Unilateral Vestibulopathy: A Prospective Observational Study.
item.description: A prospective observational study (N=92) found that an 8-week structured visuo-vestibular physical therapy program significantly improved anti-compensatory saccades (AcS) in patients with acute unilateral vestibulopathy, reflecting vestibulo-saccadic interaction and central compensation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study (N=333) found that sarcopenia increases the risk of adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years following anterior cervical discectomy and fusion.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis (N=17 trials, 1,372 participants) found that shortwave diathermy significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-0.94, -0.50]; p < .01) in knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
item.description: A qualitative evidence synthesis of 22 studies, involving 645 informants, identified significant psychological and social impacts of eating and drinking difficulties after stroke, extending beyond physical impairment.
item.author: PubMed

item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated the impact of postpartum exercise on pelvic floor disorders and diastasis recti abdominis. It found that exercise positively influences the recovery and prevention of these conditions.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Improved Flexor Spasticity in Patients with Chronic Stroke Using a Dynamic Wrist-Hand Stretching Device: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A pilot RCT (n=30) found that a novel dynamic wrist-hand stretching device (Hand Stretcher®) improved flexor spasticity and hand function in chronic stroke patients, suggesting its potential for long-term management.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Efficacy and Modulating Factors of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation on Motor Function in Parkinson's Disease: A Meta-Analysis.
item.description: A meta-analysis evaluated the short-term and long-term therapeutic effects of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) on motor function in Parkinson's disease (PD), finding rTMS effective in the short term, with variable modulating factors.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: A systematic review on hamstring injury return to play (RTP) found a high recurrence rate of 57% within the first month and identified a lack of clear consensus on weighted RTP criteria.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of different exercise training modalities on 24-hour ambulatory blood pressure in adults with hypertension: a network meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials
item.description: A network meta-analysis of 31 RCTs (n=1345) compared the effects of different exercise training modalities on 24-hour ambulatory blood pressure in adults with hypertension, finding combined training effective.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Preoperative physiotherapy and one-year patient-reported outcomes after primary total knee arthroplasty: a registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients.
item.description: A registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients found no significant difference in patient-reported outcomes or pain at 2 months and 1 year post-total knee arthroplasty between those who received preoperative physiotherapy and those who did not.
item.author: PubMed
